scores.metric: sandbox --disable all (masked). 3 diffs = the la-addend fold + the 2 (lbu/sb) offset re-encodings. Full-build SHA1 is the real gate.

sessions.lever: blocked-lane triage — 2 more rules-removed full-build variants, both DIFF

reviewer.evidence: No improving candidate — candidate.c is HEAD's body (matches only with the 3 rules). No new cheat.

remaining_gap.summary: cc1 combine-pass single-displacement addressing fold. Target encodes the 0x415 displacement in the load/store OFFSET (`la(D_80101EC8)` + `lbu/sb 1045(reg)`); cc1 folds &D_80101EC8 + 0x415 into the `la` symbol+addend (`la(D_80101EC8+1045)` + `lbu/sb 0(reg)`). Identical runtime memory access, different MIPS operand encoding. The function has exactly ONE displacement (0x415) so cc1's combine has no degree of freedom to keep base + offset separate — proven by the SIBLING func_8005509C which MATCHES because it has TWO displacements (0x414+0x415) forcing base separation.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: func_800550E8 in regfix.txt:2897-2899 (3 reciprocal substs: la addend strip @8, lbu offset re-add @10, sb offset re-add @13). All 3 must go for COMPLETED-C; no single-displacement pure-C form found that stops cc1 folding 0x415 into the la.

next_hypotheses: Struct-field access: if D_80101EC8 has a known struct type with a u8 field at +0x415, accessing `obj[arg0].field` may make cc1 emit member-offset addressing (`lbu off(base)`) instead of folding the symbol+addend. Requires recovering the struct layout (0x44C stride suggests a per-entry struct). HIGH-value untried lever — needs the type.

Read GCC 2.7.2 combine.c symbol+offset fold (`PLUS (SYMBOL_REF) (CONST_INT)` -> `SYMBOL_REF+addend`): find the predicate that the sibling's two-displacement form fails (keeping base separate). Determine if a single-displacement C shape can fail the same predicate (e.g. a base whose runtime component the combine can't prove constant-foldable with the symbol).

If no pure-C form exists (single-displacement = genuinely no degree of freedom, as the analysis strongly suggests), this is a cc1-determinism wall — NOT canonical-asm (hand-coded scan LOW 0/8, ordinary byte loop) and compiler-patch is forbidden ([[no-compiler-divergence]]). Surface as a policy question: is a reciprocal-reencoding regfix (same bytes, operand-form only) acceptable here, or does it stay parked indefinitely?

rejected_forms.reason: DIFF (sha 24a6570f) — moves the +0x415 out of the loop offset entirely; wrong (target applies it per-iteration in the offset).
REGRESSED — only one real displacement; faking a second is a cheat AND worse codegen (from prior card).
